---
id: "13.5.3"
title: "Trace Completeness and Orphan Span Detection (unresolved parent_span_id, missing roots, traceparent loss, OTel collector queue overflow, head-vs-tail sampling bias, Kafka bridge truncation, Tempo and APM ingestion drops, negative-duration clock skew)"
status: "verified"
criticality: "high"
splunkPillar: "Observability"
---

# UC-13.5.3 · Trace Completeness and Orphan Span Detection (unresolved parent_span_id, missing roots, traceparent loss, OTel collector queue overflow, head-vs-tail sampling bias, Kafka bridge truncation, Tempo and APM ingestion drops, negative-duration clock skew)

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Reliability, Configuration &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We treat a distributed trace like a relay race where every runner must hand off the same baton; when someone grabs a stick with no teammate attached, or the official record loses the team number at a handoff, the race photo stops telling the truth, so we spotlight those broken handoffs before leaders argue about the wrong cause.*

---

## Description

Detects distributed trace fragmentation by correlating unresolved parent_span_id links inside trace_id graphs, traces that lack any resolvable root span in-window, missing trace identifiers consistent with lost traceparent propagation, OpenTelemetry collector queue overflow and exporter failure signals, head-sampling bias versus tail-sampling completeness, Kafka bridge lag truncation risk, negative-duration spans from clock skew, and APM path drops mirrored from Tempo, Datadog, or Splunk APM bridges. The control enriches services with inventory sampling_strategy and ownership for fast remediation.

## Value

Platform and product leaders gain defensible evidence when service graphs become misleading because spans float without parents, roots vanish at vendor boundaries, or pipelines silently drop batches, which is different from asking whether latency percentiles moved or whether error rates burned budget. Incident teams shorten mean time to innocence by separating true application regressions from broken propagation, collector saturation, or sampling policy drift, while finance partners see when cost-driven sampling changes erode forensic completeness.

## Implementation

Land spans in index=spans, collector metrics in metrics indexes, collector and tail-sampling logs in index=observability, and Kafka lag counters alongside bridge_publish totals; maintain span_inventory.csv and catalog service_inventory for joins. Save obs_uc_13_5_3_trace_completeness_orphans on a five-minute cadence with earliest=-2h@h, page when severity is critical or high after volume guards, and route by owner_team. Tune orphan_ratio gates, root_missing_count floors, and dropped_span_count coupling per environment tier.

## Evidence

Saved search obs_uc_13_5_3_trace_completeness_orphans with scheduled execution, dashboard drilldowns tied to trace explorer and collector health views, and weekly CSV exports archived to a restricted observability_evidence index.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab tenant, ingest spans with fabricated unresolved parent_span_id values inside the same trace_id while collector metrics show rising otelcol_processor_dropped_spans, confirm obs_uc_13_5_3_trace_completeness_orphans returns critical or high within two five-minute buckets with non-null owner_team after joins.

### Negative scenario

After recovery when parent links normalize, collector drops return to baseline, and kafka_consumer_lag falls, confirm severities return to low or medium for tier-one services and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to critical and high only.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control together with observability engineers, SRE leads, and service owners who answer for end-to-end trace integrity across Splunk Enterprise, Splunk Observability Cloud, Grafana Tempo, Jaeger, Zipkin, Datadog APM mirrors, and Kafka-backed trace bridges. Before you tune ratios, align on vocabulary: a trace is a directed acyclic graph of spans keyed by trace_id; a root span is one whose parent_span_id is empty, all zeros, or otherwise indicates no in-process parent; an orphan span is one whose parent_span_id does not resolve to any span_id observed inside the same trace_id in your retention window; missing traceparent semantics mean the W3C traceparent header or B3 headers failed to survive a network hop so identifiers never joined the trace; dropped_attributes_count on OTLP spans records how many attributes the SDK or collector discarded because of attribute count or size limits, which is different from dropping whole spans but still erodes investigative fidelity.

Propagation is not magic: OpenTelemetry SDKs must inject and extract context on every synchronous boundary, and you must explicitly propagate through asynchronous continuations, thread pools, reactive stacks, and language-specific task executors. W3C Trace Context defines traceparent and tracestate; B3 remains common in older meshes; baggage carries cross-cutting keys but will not repair a lost traceparent. At gRPC and HTTP ingress, reverse proxies must forward trace headers according to product documentation, including edge cases where header names are normalized or stripped. Message boundaries are frequent fracture points: Kafka, RabbitMQ, Amazon SQS, and similar brokers only preserve trace linkage when producers embed trace context into message headers or payload envelopes agreed with consumers, and schema-registry upgrades that rewrite headers can silently detach context.

Span kind correctness matters for interpreting orphan signals: CLIENT and SERVER spans should bracket RPC calls; PRODUCER and CONSUMER spans should bracket messaging; INTERNAL spans cover in-process work. Mis-labeled kinds do not always create orphan math, but they routinely confuse tail-sampling policies that key on kind or status. Third-party SaaS systems may emit child spans without a root you can see, which looks like missing roots even when the remote system is healthy; treat vendor boundaries as first-class caveats. Time skew produces negative span durations when host clocks disagree; pair this control with NTP discipline and optional clock-skew exporter metrics so skew is an infrastructure explanation rather than an application mystery.

Collector topology shapes completeness: tail-sampling components need enough memory and CPU to hold trace decisions until policies evaluate; head sampling decides early and can under-sample rare error traces unless you combine policies or add error-biased rules. QueueingExporter settings, batch processor sizes, and retry policies determine whether backpressure surfaces as otelcol_processor_dropped_spans, otelcol_exporter_send_failed_spans, or otelcol_receiver_refused_spans. Kafka bridges that translate OTLP to vendor formats can truncate oversized batches; watch kafka_consumer_lag and bridge publish counters for evidence of backlog that precedes index drops.

Legal and privacy posture: span attributes may include user identifiers, device identifiers, and free-text error strings. Hash or strip at the gateway when counsel requires minimization, and document which fields remain in each index. Role-based access control should restrict high-cardinality trace indexes to engineering identities with job need, segregate production from non-production with deployment.environment, and avoid leaking internal tenant identifiers into shared operational chats when this search returns exemplar trace identifiers during incidents.

Differentiation recap: UC-13.5.1 remains duration-first percentile anomaly detection with dwell and hotspot language; UC-13.5.2 remains error-rate-first burn-rate narratives across spans, span events, metrics, and Web lanes. This UC-13.5.3 focuses on structural integrity—orphan ratios, missing roots, dropped batches, header loss, sampling bias, bridge truncation, and clock-skew signatures—so service graphs and dependency tools stop lying in the subtle ways that latency and error alarms alone may not expose. If completeness is chronically poor, stabilize propagation and collectors before trusting fine-grained latency regressions as pure code defects.

Risk briefing for leadership: apparent fragmentation can be sampling policy, not customer pain; combine this control with business-level service level indicators and incident timelines so executives do not confuse observability pipeline thinning with application outages. Conversely, silent drops can hide real outages; treat rising dropped_span_count as seriously as rising ERROR counts when volumes correlate with customer reports.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane A lands raw spans in index=spans with sourcetypes such as otel:trace, otel:span, or splunk:apm:span after OTLP flattening. Preserve trace_id, span_id, parent_span_id, service.name, deployment.environment, span.kind, status fields, duration in nanoseconds or milliseconds, dropped_attributes_count when present, and any linkage fields your APM bridge adds. Normalize hexadecimal identifiers to lowercase at ingest to reduce join friction.

Lane B lands OpenTelemetry Collector internal metrics in metrics-class indexes with names like otelcol_exporter_send_failed_spans, otelcol_processor_dropped_spans, and otelcol_receiver_refused_spans. Ensure metric dimensions include service, pipeline, exporter, and cluster so multisearch arms can attribute drops to a gateway versus an agent.

Lane C lands collector logs in index=observability with sourcetype=otel:collector capturing export timeouts, HTTP 413 and 429 responses, retry exhaustion, queue saturation, and TLS failures. Parser-friendly JSON helps; keep raw lines for regex fallbacks when vendors embed errors in text.

Lane D lands tail-sampling decision telemetry with sourcetype=otel:tail_sampling when you export policy evaluation logs from the collector or a sidecar. Include probabilistic head sampling cues, tail policy names, and error-biased rule hits so the search can contrast head bias against tail completeness goals.

Lane E lands Kafka bridge and consumer lag telemetry in metrics indexes, including kafka_consumer_lag and bridge_published_total style counters, when traces traverse a kafka_bridge between data centers or into Splunk HTTP Event Collector. Sudden lag spikes often precede truncation or partial batch publication.

HTTP Event Collector tokens that receive OTLP or JSON spans should be rate-limited and monitored for 4xx bursts that indicate oversized payloads or authentication drift. Kubernetes workloads should expose build identifiers that map to service.version for rollbacks correlated with fragmentation spikes.

Maintain span_inventory.csv keyed on service.name with columns for environment, owner_team, and sampling_strategy so enrichment stays declarative. Maintain catalog service_inventory items for teams that store ownership only in configuration items rather than observability labels.

Example readiness checks: verify a canary service produces a three-hop trace with a single root in Search, verify tail-sampling retains ERROR traces under policy, verify collector memory limits are below OutOfMemoryKill thresholds under peak load tests, and verify traceparent survives your primary ingress controller configuration.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as obs_uc_13_5_3_trace_completeness_orphans with a five-minute schedule during steady production, dispatch earliest=-2h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is critical or high after span volume guards. Throttle duplicate service_name and environment pairs for thirty minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action and owner_team in pager narratives so responders open propagation runbooks, collector dashboards, and Kafka lag views without guesswork.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans five arms for raw spans, collector internal metrics, collector logs interpreted as drop stress, tail-sampling decision logs, and Kafka bridge lag hints. coalesce ladders unify field naming drift across OTLP flattenings and APM bridges. streamstats estimates short orphan_ratio rolling behavior per service and environment while eventstats provides environment baselines for context. case encodes severity with explicit coupling between orphan_ratio, root_missing_count proxies, missing_traceparent_count, and dropped_span_count so queue overflow cannot hide behind quiet span lanes. inputlookup span_inventory.csv and a catalog service_inventory join both resolve owner_team and declared sampling_strategy for routing. Negative duration spikes elevate severity toward medium to catch clock skew and bad exporter timestamps.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.5.3 trace completeness orphan spans missing traceparent collector queue overflow head-vs-tail sampling kafka bridge skew. Tunables: earliest=-2h@h bucket=5m critical_orphan_ratio=0.05 critical_root_missing_per_5m=50")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=spans (sourcetype=otel:trace OR sourcetype=otel:span OR sourcetype=splunk:apm:span) earliest=-2h@h latest=now
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name','resource.attributes.service.name',service_name,""))))
      | eval environment=coalesce('deployment.environment',deployment_environment,"unknown_environment")
      | eval trace_id=coalesce(trace_id,traceId,"")
      | eval span_id=coalesce(span_id,spanId,id,"")
      | eval parent_span_id=coalesce(parent_span_id,'parent_span_id',parentSpanId,"")
      | eval parent_span_id=coalesce(nullif(trim(parent_span_id),""),"0000000000000000")
      | eval span_kind=coalesce('span.kind',span_kind,"INTERNAL")
      | eval sampling_decision=coalesce('sampling.decision',sampling_decision,"unsampled_unknown")
      | eval duration_ms=tonumber(coalesce(duration_ms,round('duration.nanos'/1000000,4),round(duration_ns/1000000,4),0))
      | eval neg_duration=if(duration_ms<0,1,0)
      | eval missing_traceparent=if(len(trace_id)<8 OR match(trace_id,"^0+$") OR match(trace_id,"^unknown"),1,0)
      | eval is_root=if(parent_span_id=="" OR parent_span_id=="0000000000000000" OR match(parent_span_id,"^0+$"),1,0)
      | bin _time span=5m
      | eventstats values(span_id) AS span_mv BY trace_id _time
      | eval orphan_span=if(is_root==0 AND mvcount(span_mv)>0 AND isnull(mvfind(span_mv,"^".parent_span_id."$")),1,0)
      | eventstats sum(eval(is_root)) AS roots_in_trace BY trace_id _time
      | eval rootless_row=if(roots_in_trace==0,1,0)
      | stats count AS spans_total sum(eval(is_root)) AS root_span_count sum(eval(orphan_span)) AS orphan_span_count sum(eval(missing_traceparent)) AS missing_traceparent_count sum(eval(rootless_row)) AS root_missing_count sum(eval(neg_duration)) AS neg_duration_spans perc95(duration_ms) AS p95_trace_duration_ms BY _time service_name environment ]
    [ search (index=metrics OR index=observability_metrics OR index=otel_metrics) earliest=-2h@h latest=now ("otelcol_exporter_send_failed_spans" OR "otelcol_processor_dropped_spans" OR "otelcol_receiver_refused_spans")
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service,service_name,"otel_collector_gateway"))))
      | eval environment=coalesce(deployment_environment,environment,"unknown_environment")
      | eval dropped_span_count=tonumber(coalesce(_value,value,metric_value,0))
      | eval top_dropped_reason=coalesce(metric_name,name,"otelcol_metric_drop")
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats sum(dropped_span_count) AS dropped_span_count values(top_dropped_reason) AS drm BY _time service_name environment
      | eval spans_total=0 | eval root_span_count=0 | eval orphan_span_count=0 | eval missing_traceparent_count=0 | eval root_missing_count=0 | eval p95_trace_duration_ms=0 | eval neg_duration_spans=0 ]
    [ search index=observability sourcetype=otel:collector earliest=-2h@h latest=now (queue OR backpressure OR timeout OR refused OR overflow OR "413" OR "429" OR "503")
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(component,service_name,"otel_collector"))))
      | eval environment=coalesce(deployment_environment,environment,"unknown_environment")
      | eval dropped_span_count=if(match(lower(_raw),"overflow|refused|queue.*full|exporter.*fail|timeout|backpressure|retry"),1,0)
      | eval top_dropped_reason=case(match(lower(_raw),"overflow"),"queue_overflow",match(lower(_raw),"timeout"),"export_timeout",match(lower(_raw),"refused"),"receiver_refused",true(),"collector_log_stress")
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats sum(dropped_span_count) AS dropped_span_count values(top_dropped_reason) AS drm BY _time service_name environment
      | eval spans_total=0 | eval root_span_count=0 | eval orphan_span_count=0 | eval missing_traceparent_count=0 | eval root_missing_count=0 | eval p95_trace_duration_ms=0 | eval neg_duration_spans=0 ]
    [ search index=observability sourcetype=otel:tail_sampling earliest=-2h@h latest=now
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(collector_cluster,service_name,"otel_tail_sampling"))))
      | eval environment=coalesce(environment,"unknown_environment")
      | eval sampling_decision=coalesce(decision,policy,"tail_policy_unparsed")
      | eval head_bias_flag=if(match(lower(_raw),"probabilistic|always_off|parentbased|head.*sampl") AND NOT match(lower(_raw),"tail"),1,0)
      | eval dropped_span_count=0
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats max(head_bias_flag) AS head_bias_flag values(sampling_decision) AS sampling_strategy_mv BY _time service_name environment
      | eval spans_total=0 | eval root_span_count=0 | eval orphan_span_count=0 | eval missing_traceparent_count=0 | eval root_missing_count=0 | eval p95_trace_duration_ms=0 | eval neg_duration_spans=0 | eval drm=null ]
    [ search (index=metrics OR index=observability_metrics) earliest=-2h@h latest=now ("kafka_consumer_lag" OR "bridge_published_total")
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(topic,consumer_group,service_name,"kafka_bridge"))))
      | eval environment=coalesce(environment,"unknown_environment")
      | eval lagv=tonumber(coalesce(_value,value,0))
      | eval dropped_span_count=if(match(lower(metric_name),"lag") AND lagv>200000,1,0)
      | eval top_dropped_reason=if(match(lower(metric_name),"lag"),"kafka_bridge_truncation_risk","bridge_publish_total")
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats sum(dropped_span_count) AS dropped_span_count values(top_dropped_reason) AS drm BY _time service_name environment
      | eval spans_total=0 | eval root_span_count=0 | eval orphan_span_count=0 | eval missing_traceparent_count=0 | eval root_missing_count=0 | eval p95_trace_duration_ms=0 | eval neg_duration_spans=0 ]
| eval service_name=coalesce(service_name,"unknown_service")
| eval environment=coalesce(environment,"unknown_environment")
| stats sum(spans_total) AS spans_total sum(root_span_count) AS root_span_count sum(orphan_span_count) AS orphan_span_count sum(missing_traceparent_count) AS missing_traceparent_count sum(dropped_span_count) AS dropped_span_count sum(root_missing_count) AS root_missing_count sum(neg_duration_spans) AS neg_duration_spans max(p95_trace_duration_ms) AS p95_trace_duration_ms values(drm) AS drop_reason_mv values(sampling_strategy_mv) AS strat_mv max(head_bias_flag) AS head_bias_flag
    BY _time service_name environment
| eval sampling_strategy=coalesce(mvindex(strat_mv,0),if(head_bias_flag==1,"head_parent_based","tail_or_unknown"))
| eval sampling_strategy=coalesce(sampling_strategy,"unspecified")
| eval orphan_ratio=round(orphan_span_count/nullif(spans_total,0),4)
| eval completeness_pct=round(100*(1-(orphan_span_count+missing_traceparent_count*0.25+root_missing_count*0.1)/nullif(spans_total+dropped_span_count,0)),2)
| streamstats window=6 current=t global=f avg(orphan_ratio) AS orphan_ratio_roll BY service_name environment
| eventstats avg(orphan_ratio) AS env_orphan_baseline BY environment
| eval orphan_ratio=coalesce(orphan_ratio_roll,orphan_ratio,0)
| eval top_dropped_reason=coalesce(mvindex(mvdedup(drop_reason_mv),0),"none_observed")
| eval severity=case(orphan_ratio>0.05 AND root_missing_count>=50 AND spans_total>=200,"critical",orphan_ratio>0.05 AND spans_total>=200,"high",missing_traceparent_count>50 OR dropped_span_count>25,"high",orphan_ratio>0.02,"medium",true(),"low")
| eval severity=case(neg_duration_spans>0 AND severity=="low","medium",true(),severity)
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical","halt_rollout_fix_propagation_validate_w3c_b3_baggage_and_tail_sampler_capacity",severity=="high","inspect_otel_collector_queues_exporters_and_kafka_bridge_lag_then_validate_traceparent_hops",severity=="medium","tune_sampling_policies_review_async_context_and_third_party_roots",true(),"observe_baseline_compare_inventory_sampling_strategy")
| rename service_name AS "service.name"
| join type=left "service.name" [| inputlookup span_inventory.csv | eval environment=coalesce(env,environment) | fields "service.name" environment sampling_strategy owner_team ]
| join type=left "service.name" [ search index=catalog sourcetype=service_inventory earliest=-24h latest=now | dedup service.name | table service.name owner_team ]
| eval service_name='service.name'
| eval environment=coalesce(environment,environment)
| eval sampling_strategy=coalesce(sampling_strategy,sampling_strategy)
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unassigned_platform")
| table _time service_name environment sampling_strategy spans_total root_span_count orphan_span_count missing_traceparent_count dropped_span_count completeness_pct orphan_ratio top_dropped_reason severity recommended_action owner_team
```

savedsearches.conf sketch for packaging teams:

```ini
[obs_uc_13_5_3_trace_completeness_orphans_alert]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -2h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch obs_uc_13_5_3_trace_completeness_orphans | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance guidance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time climbing, materialize each lane into five-minute summaries keyed on service_name and environment, widen alerts to read summaries with earliest=-90m@m, and retain this full search for investigations. For very large fleets, split alerts by environment using lookup tier columns.

### Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in your trace explorer: pick a known multi-hop flow, enumerate span_id and parent_span_id pairs inside one trace_id, and confirm Splunk orphan counts align with manual graph inspection for the same window. If Splunk shows orphans while the UI does not, verify indexer delays, field extractions, and case sensitivity on hexadecimal identifiers.

Validate collector metrics arms by inducing a harmless export failure in a lab gateway and confirming otelcol_exporter_send_failed_spans rises with expected dimensions. Validate log arms by generating a queue overflow message and confirming top_dropped_reason resolves to queue_overflow tokens.

Validate header-loss hypotheses by capturing ingress and sidecar access logs alongside spans; when traceparent disappears at a hop, missing_traceparent_count should correlate with that hop rather than application deploys.

Validate inventory joins by running inputlookup span_inventory.csv alone and confirming keys cover tier-zero services. Validate catalog joins by ensuring deduped service.name rows exist for newly onboarded workloads.

Synthetic drill: inject spans with fabricated parent_span_id values that do not exist in the same trace in non-production, confirm severity escalates within two buckets, then restore instrumentation and confirm decay.

Negative test: on a calm window without collector changes, high-volume services should remain below critical thresholds unless baselines are miscalibrated; if noise persists, raise spans_total floors inside case logic.

Runbook linkage: tie results to W3C propagation checklists, Kafka header preservation guides, collector sizing calculators, and Grafana Tempo TraceQL queries that highlight structurally broken traces.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Missing W3C propagation through a gRPC-aware reverse proxy: compare ingress headers with upstream service logs, enable explicit trace header allow lists, and verify the proxy does not strip traceparent when translating HTTP/2 pseudo-headers.

Case 2 — Kafka producer dropping trace headers on schema-registry mismatch: inspect serializer configuration, confirm magic-byte and schema-id headers still carry baggage fields, and replay a canary message with embedded trace context through the consumer path.

Case 3 — Tail-sampling collector out-of-memory events dropping batches: raise memory limits, shrink max queue size with backpressure alerts, shard collectors by tenant, and validate policy windows against peak trace fan-out.

Case 4 — Asynchronous worker pool producing detached spans: fix context attachment on task submission, adopt OpenTelemetry context wrappers for your runtime, and verify thread-local carriers are cleared between jobs.

Case 5 — Library upgrade silently losing implicit context carriers: bisect dependencies, read release notes for breaking async hooks, and run shadow traffic in staging with strict parent resolution checks.

Case 6 — Browser real user monitoring beacon arriving after the server span export window: align clock skew, extend tail-sampling wait where legal, and separate web from service inventories for alerting.

Case 7 — Sidecar mTLS rejecting OTLP batches: renew certificates, align SANs, and confirm mesh policy allows OTLP gRPC from application pods to collector namespaces.

Case 8 — NTP drift producing negative span durations: remediate time sync on nodes, filter neg_duration_spans in dashboards until fixed, and pair with clock-skew exporter metrics for proof.

Case 9 — Org-wide sampling cut to one percent for cost control elevating statistical noise: document acceptance thresholds, add error-biased policies, and communicate reduced fidelity to incident commanders.

Case 10 — Cross-cluster trace splitting after ingress NAT rewrites: stabilize service identity attributes, align cluster tags, and avoid collapsing distinct clusters into one inventory row.

Case 11 — kafka_bridge index retention drop hiding historical tails: restore retention or export cold archives before comparing completeness week over week.

Case 12 — Head sampling biased away from rare error traces: move error retention to tail policies, increase probabilistic rates for tier-zero paths, and validate with Honeycomb-style tail sampling guidance in staging.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, join, and inputlookup appear; final table lists service, environment, sampling, completeness, orphan_ratio, drop reason, severity, recommended_action, and owner_team.



## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.5.3 trace completeness orphan spans missing traceparent collector queue overflow head-vs-tail sampling kafka bridge skew. Tunables: earliest=-2h@h bucket=5m critical_orphan_ratio=0.05 critical_root_missing_per_5m=50")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=spans (sourcetype=otel:trace OR sourcetype=otel:span OR sourcetype=splunk:apm:span) earliest=-2h@h latest=now
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name','resource.attributes.service.name',service_name,""))))
      | eval environment=coalesce('deployment.environment',deployment_environment,"unknown_environment")
      | eval trace_id=coalesce(trace_id,traceId,"")
      | eval span_id=coalesce(span_id,spanId,id,"")
      | eval parent_span_id=coalesce(parent_span_id,'parent_span_id',parentSpanId,"")
      | eval parent_span_id=coalesce(nullif(trim(parent_span_id),""),"0000000000000000")
      | eval span_kind=coalesce('span.kind',span_kind,"INTERNAL")
      | eval sampling_decision=coalesce('sampling.decision',sampling_decision,"unsampled_unknown")
      | eval duration_ms=tonumber(coalesce(duration_ms,round('duration.nanos'/1000000,4),round(duration_ns/1000000,4),0))
      | eval neg_duration=if(duration_ms<0,1,0)
      | eval missing_traceparent=if(len(trace_id)<8 OR match(trace_id,"^0+$") OR match(trace_id,"^unknown"),1,0)
      | eval is_root=if(parent_span_id=="" OR parent_span_id=="0000000000000000" OR match(parent_span_id,"^0+$"),1,0)
      | bin _time span=5m
      | eventstats values(span_id) AS span_mv BY trace_id _time
      | eval orphan_span=if(is_root==0 AND mvcount(span_mv)>0 AND isnull(mvfind(span_mv,"^".parent_span_id."$")),1,0)
      | eventstats sum(eval(is_root)) AS roots_in_trace BY trace_id _time
      | eval rootless_row=if(roots_in_trace==0,1,0)
      | stats count AS spans_total sum(eval(is_root)) AS root_span_count sum(eval(orphan_span)) AS orphan_span_count sum(eval(missing_traceparent)) AS missing_traceparent_count sum(eval(rootless_row)) AS root_missing_count sum(eval(neg_duration)) AS neg_duration_spans perc95(duration_ms) AS p95_trace_duration_ms BY _time service_name environment ]
    [ search (index=metrics OR index=observability_metrics OR index=otel_metrics) earliest=-2h@h latest=now ("otelcol_exporter_send_failed_spans" OR "otelcol_processor_dropped_spans" OR "otelcol_receiver_refused_spans")
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service,service_name,"otel_collector_gateway"))))
      | eval environment=coalesce(deployment_environment,environment,"unknown_environment")
      | eval dropped_span_count=tonumber(coalesce(_value,value,metric_value,0))
      | eval top_dropped_reason=coalesce(metric_name,name,"otelcol_metric_drop")
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats sum(dropped_span_count) AS dropped_span_count values(top_dropped_reason) AS drm BY _time service_name environment
      | eval spans_total=0 | eval root_span_count=0 | eval orphan_span_count=0 | eval missing_traceparent_count=0 | eval root_missing_count=0 | eval p95_trace_duration_ms=0 | eval neg_duration_spans=0 ]
    [ search index=observability sourcetype=otel:collector earliest=-2h@h latest=now (queue OR backpressure OR timeout OR refused OR overflow OR "413" OR "429" OR "503")
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(component,service_name,"otel_collector"))))
      | eval environment=coalesce(deployment_environment,environment,"unknown_environment")
      | eval dropped_span_count=if(match(lower(_raw),"overflow|refused|queue.*full|exporter.*fail|timeout|backpressure|retry"),1,0)
      | eval top_dropped_reason=case(match(lower(_raw),"overflow"),"queue_overflow",match(lower(_raw),"timeout"),"export_timeout",match(lower(_raw),"refused"),"receiver_refused",true(),"collector_log_stress")
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats sum(dropped_span_count) AS dropped_span_count values(top_dropped_reason) AS drm BY _time service_name environment
      | eval spans_total=0 | eval root_span_count=0 | eval orphan_span_count=0 | eval missing_traceparent_count=0 | eval root_missing_count=0 | eval p95_trace_duration_ms=0 | eval neg_duration_spans=0 ]
    [ search index=observability sourcetype=otel:tail_sampling earliest=-2h@h latest=now
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(collector_cluster,service_name,"otel_tail_sampling"))))
      | eval environment=coalesce(environment,"unknown_environment")
      | eval sampling_decision=coalesce(decision,policy,"tail_policy_unparsed")
      | eval head_bias_flag=if(match(lower(_raw),"probabilistic|always_off|parentbased|head.*sampl") AND NOT match(lower(_raw),"tail"),1,0)
      | eval dropped_span_count=0
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats max(head_bias_flag) AS head_bias_flag values(sampling_decision) AS sampling_strategy_mv BY _time service_name environment
      | eval spans_total=0 | eval root_span_count=0 | eval orphan_span_count=0 | eval missing_traceparent_count=0 | eval root_missing_count=0 | eval p95_trace_duration_ms=0 | eval neg_duration_spans=0 | eval drm=null ]
    [ search (index=metrics OR index=observability_metrics) earliest=-2h@h latest=now ("kafka_consumer_lag" OR "bridge_published_total")
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(topic,consumer_group,service_name,"kafka_bridge"))))
      | eval environment=coalesce(environment,"unknown_environment")
      | eval lagv=tonumber(coalesce(_value,value,0))
      | eval dropped_span_count=if(match(lower(metric_name),"lag") AND lagv>200000,1,0)
      | eval top_dropped_reason=if(match(lower(metric_name),"lag"),"kafka_bridge_truncation_risk","bridge_publish_total")
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats sum(dropped_span_count) AS dropped_span_count values(top_dropped_reason) AS drm BY _time service_name environment
      | eval spans_total=0 | eval root_span_count=0 | eval orphan_span_count=0 | eval missing_traceparent_count=0 | eval root_missing_count=0 | eval p95_trace_duration_ms=0 | eval neg_duration_spans=0 ]
| eval service_name=coalesce(service_name,"unknown_service")
| eval environment=coalesce(environment,"unknown_environment")
| stats sum(spans_total) AS spans_total sum(root_span_count) AS root_span_count sum(orphan_span_count) AS orphan_span_count sum(missing_traceparent_count) AS missing_traceparent_count sum(dropped_span_count) AS dropped_span_count sum(root_missing_count) AS root_missing_count sum(neg_duration_spans) AS neg_duration_spans max(p95_trace_duration_ms) AS p95_trace_duration_ms values(drm) AS drop_reason_mv values(sampling_strategy_mv) AS strat_mv max(head_bias_flag) AS head_bias_flag
    BY _time service_name environment
| eval sampling_strategy=coalesce(mvindex(strat_mv,0),if(head_bias_flag==1,"head_parent_based","tail_or_unknown"))
| eval sampling_strategy=coalesce(sampling_strategy,"unspecified")
| eval orphan_ratio=round(orphan_span_count/nullif(spans_total,0),4)
| eval completeness_pct=round(100*(1-(orphan_span_count+missing_traceparent_count*0.25+root_missing_count*0.1)/nullif(spans_total+dropped_span_count,0)),2)
| streamstats window=6 current=t global=f avg(orphan_ratio) AS orphan_ratio_roll BY service_name environment
| eventstats avg(orphan_ratio) AS env_orphan_baseline BY environment
| eval orphan_ratio=coalesce(orphan_ratio_roll,orphan_ratio,0)
| eval top_dropped_reason=coalesce(mvindex(mvdedup(drop_reason_mv),0),"none_observed")
| eval severity=case(orphan_ratio>0.05 AND root_missing_count>=50 AND spans_total>=200,"critical",orphan_ratio>0.05 AND spans_total>=200,"high",missing_traceparent_count>50 OR dropped_span_count>25,"high",orphan_ratio>0.02,"medium",true(),"low")
| eval severity=case(neg_duration_spans>0 AND severity=="low","medium",true(),severity)
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical","halt_rollout_fix_propagation_validate_w3c_b3_baggage_and_tail_sampler_capacity",severity=="high","inspect_otel_collector_queues_exporters_and_kafka_bridge_lag_then_validate_traceparent_hops",severity=="medium","tune_sampling_policies_review_async_context_and_third_party_roots",true(),"observe_baseline_compare_inventory_sampling_strategy")
| rename service_name AS "service.name"
| join type=left "service.name" [| inputlookup span_inventory.csv | eval environment=coalesce(env,environment) | fields "service.name" environment sampling_strategy owner_team ]
| join type=left "service.name" [ search index=catalog sourcetype=service_inventory earliest=-24h latest=now | dedup service.name | table service.name owner_team ]
| eval service_name='service.name'
| eval environment=coalesce(environment,environment)
| eval sampling_strategy=coalesce(sampling_strategy,sampling_strategy)
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unassigned_platform")
| table _time service_name environment sampling_strategy spans_total root_span_count orphan_span_count missing_traceparent_count dropped_span_count completeness_pct orphan_ratio top_dropped_reason severity recommended_action owner_team
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Application_State.info) AS app_info latest(Application_State.severity) AS app_sev count FROM datamodel=Application_State WHERE nodename=Application_State earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Application_State.dest Application_State.app
| rename Application_State.dest AS service_key Application_State.app AS app_key
| join type=left max=0 service_key [| tstats summariesonly=t perc95(Performance.total_processor_time) AS p95_cpu FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Performance.process | rename Performance.process AS service_key]
| table service_key app_key app_info app_sev p95_cpu
```

## Visualization

Heatmap of orphan_ratio by service_name and environment, single-value tiles for critical severity counts, timeline of dropped_span_count paired with kafka_consumer_lag, drilldown table keyed on top_dropped_reason with recommended_action, side panel for otel:collector stress lines in the same window.

## Known False Positives

Intentionally unsampled traces in lower environments can look catastrophically incomplete compared with production; segregate deployment.environment and inventory tiers before paging application owners. Third-party SaaS integrations that never export a root span you can read will inflate root-missing style counts even when customer journeys succeed; model vendor hops as external roots with documentation rather than auto-rollback. Batch jobs that legitimately emit leaf spans without a durable root by design should carry workflow attributes and inventory exclusions so nightly work does not page checkout on-call. Partial instrumentation rollouts during canary weeks routinely elevate orphan_ratio until every service adopts the same propagation library; track rollout metadata and downgrade severity for labeled canary fleets. OpenTelemetry collector autoscale events can briefly spike drops during cold start; require sustained dropped_span_count dwell and cross-lane collector metrics before declaring incidents. Rare always-on sampling tests that fabricate broken parent links for chaos drills will trip structural detectors; tie experiments to labeled namespaces. Dual-write bridges that duplicate spans with different identifier casings can create false orphan math until normalization lands; reconcile field transforms. Security proxies that scrub trace headers for privacy can look like application bugs; validate policy intent before rewriting microservice code. Index maintenance windows that shrink searchable history can make traces look suddenly fragmented when older parents age out; align retention communications with completeness dashboards.
